name: CV Tailor + Cover Letter (easybits) – V2 after feedback

parameters.formTitle: Tailor my CV for this job
parameters.formDescription: Upload the job posting (PDF, image, or screenshot). I'll rewrite your CV bullets and draft a cover letter – both in the posting's language, both ready to paste.

parameters.completionTitle: Done – your tailored application is ready
parameters.completionMessage:
=

Match score: {{ $('Assemble Document').first().json.score_before }}% → {{ $('Assemble Document').first().json.score_after }}% (+{{ $('Assemble Document').first().json.score_delta }})

Google Doc: Open your tailored application

{{ $('Assemble Document').first().json.gaps && $('Assemble Document').first().json.gaps.length ? 'Gaps to address in interview: ' + $('Assemble Document').first().json.gaps.join(', ') : 'No gaps detected — your CV covers all explicit must-haves.' }}

parameters.content:
## 📥 Job Posting Upload
Hosts a web form with a file field for the job posting (PDF, PNG, JPEG) and an optional notes field for emphasis or context (e.g., "emphasize my Python experience"). The file is passed as binary directly to the Prep Data node.

## 📋 Load Master CV
Reads every row of the `Master CV` tab from your Google Sheet – one row per experience, with `role`, `company`, `dates`, `bullets`, and `skills` columns.

## 🧰 Prep Data
Bundles the form input (job posting binary + notes) and the loaded CV into one clean object so every downstream node can reference everything from a single place. The binary key is normalized to `job_posting` for the Extractor.

## 🧠 Extract Job Posting
Sends the uploaded posting to the easybits Extractor with 10 fields configured: job_title, company, seniority, must_have_skills, nice_to_have_skills, responsibilities, keywords, tone, language, and location. Response is wrapped in a `data` object.

## 📊 Score: Before
Deterministic keyword + must-have overlap, calculated in JavaScript – no LLM involved. Must-haves count double because they're explicitly required by the posting. Same formula runs again later against the tailored bullets, so the delta reflects real keyword surfacing.

## ✍️ Tailor Bullets
First Gemini call. Rewrites the CV bullets to truthfully emphasize overlap with the posting's must-haves, mirrors the employer's phrasing where genuine experience matches, and flags real gaps in a `gaps` array. Output is in the posting's language and tone.

## 📈 Score: After
Same scoring formula as Score: Before, run against Gemini's tailored bullets. The delta is what makes the result demo-worthy – and because the calculation is deterministic, the improvement is visibly traceable to which specific keywords got surfaced.

## 💌 Cover Letter
Second Gemini call. Drafts a 3-paragraph letter (~250 words) referencing only the tailored CV – never inventing experience, never claiming gap skills. Opens with a specific tie to the company, maps experiences to must-haves, closes with a concrete next step. Language and tone match the posting.

## 📄 Assemble Document
Builds the final document body: tailored CV section on top (with score header and gaps list), cover letter below. Doc title is set from company + role for easy filing.

## 📄 Create Google Doc
Creates an empty doc with the right title (company + role) in your chosen Drive folder. Outputs the new doc's `id` for the next step.

## ✅ Completion Screen
HTML completion page showing the score delta, a clickable link to the generated Google Doc, and any gaps surfaced by Gemini. The Doc link is constructed from the file ID using the standard Google Docs URL pattern.

# 📝 CV Tailor + Cover Letter
(powered by easybits)

Upload a job posting → get a tailored CV section + matching cover letter in a Google Doc, with a before/after match score and an honest list of skills you're missing. Nothing gets invented – genuine gaps land in a `gaps` array instead of being faked into bullets.

## How It Works
Upload posting → easybits extracts structured fields → deterministic match score (before) → Gemini rewrites bullets → match score (after) → Gemini drafts cover letter → both written to a Google Doc.

## Setup

1. **Run the CV Onboarding workflow first** – it populates the Master CV Google Sheet this workflow reads from.
2. **easybits Extractor** – on n8n Cloud it's built in; self-hosted, install `@easybits/n8n-nodes-extractor` via Settings → Community Nodes. Free API key at easybits.tech.
3. **Connect credentials** – Google Sheets (Load Master CV), Gemini (both Gemini nodes, model `gemini-2.5-pro`), Google Docs (both Doc nodes, pick a target folder).
4. **Replace placeholders** – your Sheet ID in Load Master CV, your folder in Create Google Doc.
5. **Activate** and submit the form.

## Extractor Fields (10 – fits the free plan)
Paste a description into each field's description box:

- **`job_title`** *(string)* – exact title as written; null if not stated.
- **`company`** *(string)* – hiring company name; null if not named.
- **`seniority`** *(string)* – intern / junior / mid / senior / lead / principal / executive.
- **`must_have_skills`** *(array)* – required skills as SHORT noun phrases (1–3 words). Break compounds into atoms ("testing, monitoring, profiling" → 3 items). Exclude soft skills.
- **`nice_to_have_skills`** *(array)* – preferred/bonus skills, same format.
- **`responsibilities`** *(array)* – concrete duties as short action phrases, max 8.
- **`keywords`** *(array)* – domain terms worth mirroring in a CV, max 15. No generic fluff.
- **`tone`** *(string)* – formal / professional / casual / technical.
- **`language`** *(string)* – ISO 639-1 code (en, de, nl…).
- **`location`** *(string)* – remote / hybrid / city; null if not stated.

## Notes
- **Honesty guardrail.** Gemini never invents experience – missing must-haves surface as gaps.
- **Deterministic scoring.** Match score is JavaScript, not an LLM. Same formula runs before and after, so the delta reflects real keyword surfacing. Must-haves count double.
- **Language & tone** come from the Extractor – a German posting yields a German output automatically.

## 🧱 Aggregate CV Rows
Combines all the individual experience rows into a single list, so the rest of the workflow can reference the full CV from one place.

## 📝 Write to Google Doc
Inserts the assembled body text (tailored CV + cover letter) into the doc created in the previous step, targeting the same `id`.
